fact: Honey never spoils. Archaeologists found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs and it was still edible.
A bolt of lightning is five times hotter than the surface of the Sun.
Octopuses have three hearts, blue blood, and nine brains — one central brain and one in each arm.
Bananas are slightly radioactive because they contain potassium-40, a naturally occurring radioactive isotope.
The human body contains enough iron to make a nail about 3 inches long.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus — it rotates so slowly that it completes an orbit before finishing a spin.
Trees can communicate with each other through underground fungal networks called the 'Wood Wide Web'.
Water can boil and freeze at the same time. It's called the 'triple point' and happens at exactly 0.01°C.
A group of flamingos is called a 'flamboyance'. A group of owls is called a 'parliament'.
The Mantis shrimp can punch with the force of a bullet and sees 16 types of colour receptors — humans only have 3.
Sharks are older than trees. Sharks have existed for about 450 million years; trees appeared about 350 million years ago.
The footprints left on the Moon by Apollo astronauts will remain for at least 100 million years — there's no wind to erase them.
A single cloud can weigh more than a million pounds but stays aloft because its water droplets are so tiny.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramid.
Wombat droppings are cube-shaped — the only animal in the world known to produce cubic faeces.
India invented the number zero. The concept was formalised by the mathematician Brahmagupta around 628 CE.
The game of Chess was invented in India. It was originally called 'Chaturanga' and dates back to the 6th century.
India has the largest number of post offices in the world — over 1.5 lakh post offices across the country.
Shampoo was invented in India. The word comes from the Hindi word 'champo' meaning to massage.
The Taj Mahal changes colour throughout the day — pinkish at dawn, white in the afternoon, and golden at night.
India is the world's largest producer of milk and the largest consumer of gold.
The Indian Railways is one of the largest employers in the world with over 1.3 million employees.
Yoga is over 5,000 years old and originated in India. Today over 300 million people practise it worldwide.
India's ISRO reached Mars on its very first attempt in 2013 — the only country in history to do so.
The Kumbh Mela gathering in India is so large it is visible from space — it's the world's largest human gathering.
Sanskrit is considered the mother of many modern languages. It is one of the most precisely structured languages ever created.
India has the world's largest school — the City Montessori School in Lucknow has over 56,000 students.
If you shuffle a deck of 52 cards, the exact order has almost certainly never existed before in the history of the universe.
The number 1 is not considered a prime number, even though it has no factors other than itself.
A Googol is the number 1 followed by 100 zeros. A Googolplex is 1 followed by a Googol of zeros — too big to write.
If you multiply 111,111,111 by 111,111,111 you get 12,345,678,987,654,321 — a perfect mountain of digits.
The Fibonacci sequence (1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8…) appears in sunflower seeds, pine cones, and even galaxies.
There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than there are atoms in the known observable universe.
Zero is the only number that cannot be represented in Roman numerals.
Your eyes can distinguish about 10 million different colours, but your brain can only name about 30 of them.
The human nose can detect over 1 trillion different smells — far more than the 10,000 scientists believed for decades.
Your bones are about 5 times stronger than steel of the same density. Bone tissue constantly rebuilds itself.
The human brain uses about 20% of the body's total energy even though it only makes up 2% of your body weight.
Your stomach gets a completely new lining every 3-4 days. The acid inside would otherwise digest the stomach itself.
Humans are the only animals that blush. Charles Darwin called it 'the most peculiar and most human of all expressions'.
There are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on all of Earth's beaches combined.
If you removed all the empty space from atoms in every human, all 8 billion of us would fit in a sugar cube.
The Sun's light takes 8 minutes to reach Earth, but it takes 100,000 years to travel from the core to the surface.
Neutron stars are so dense that a teaspoonful of their material would weigh about a billion tonnes.
Saturn is so light relative to its size that it would float in water — it is the least dense planet in our solar system.
There is a giant cloud of alcohol floating in the constellation Sagittarius containing enough ethanol to fill trillions of pints.
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire. Teaching started at Oxford around 1096; the Aztec Empire began in 1428.
Napoleon was once attacked by rabbits. He ordered a rabbit hunt, but the rabbits were tame and swarmed him for food.
The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye — that's a myth. It was confirmed by Chinese astronaut Yang Liwei.
Vikings never actually wore horned helmets. That image was invented by a costume designer for an 1876 opera.
The first computer bug was a real bug — a moth was found stuck in a relay of the Harvard Mark II computer in 1947.
Email is older than the World Wide Web. The first email was sent in 1971; the web was invented in 1991.
The first mobile phone call was made in 1973. The phone weighed 1.1 kg and took 10 hours to charge for 30 minutes of talk time.
The programming language Python is named after the comedy group Monty Python, not the snake.
Nintendo was founded in 1889 — originally as a playing card company in Kyoto, Japan.
A single mature tree can absorb up to 22 kg of CO₂ per year and produce enough oxygen for 2 people.
The Amazon rainforest produces about 20% of the world's oxygen and is called 'the lungs of the Earth'.
A single lightning bolt carries enough electricity to power a 100-watt bulb for about 3 months.
Glaciers store about 69% of the world's fresh water. If they all melted, sea levels would rise by about 70 metres.
The world's oldest living organism is a Posidonia seagrass meadow in Australia estimated to be 200,000 years old.
Sound travels about 4 times faster through water than through air.
The loudest sound ever recorded was the 1883 eruption of Krakatoa — heard 4,800 km away in Australia.
Cats always land on their feet due to a reflex called 'aerial righting'. They can survive falls from great heights.
Glass is technically a liquid — an extremely slow-moving amorphous solid that flows at room temperature over centuries.
A day on Mercury lasts longer than a year on Mercury because it rotates so slowly while orbiting the Sun quickly.
The tongue is not divided into sections for different tastes. The 'taste map' taught in textbooks was a mistranslation.
A group of crows is called a 'murder'. A group of jellyfish is called a 'smack'. A group of cats is called a 'clowder'.
The average person walks about 1,00,000 km in their lifetime — roughly 2.5 times around the Earth.
Butterflies taste with their feet. Sensors on their legs help them identify food and suitable plants for laying eggs.
Cold water boils faster than hot water under certain conditions — a phenomenon known as the Mpemba Effect.
India is home to 17% of the world's population but only has 2.4% of the world's land area.
The bindi worn on the forehead has roots in the concept of the 'third eye' — representing wisdom and intuition in Indian culture.
Diamonds were first mined in India. For over 1,000 years, India was the world's only source of diamonds.
The Indian mathematician Aryabhata calculated the value of Pi to 4 decimal places in 499 CE — over a thousand years ago.
India's 'Valley of Flowers' in Uttarakhand has over 500 species of wildflowers and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
In a room of just 23 people, there is a 50% chance that two of them share the same birthday.
Pi has been calculated to over 100 trillion decimal places and never repeats or ends.
The word 'hundred' comes from the Old Norse 'hundrath' which actually meant 120, not 100.
All odd numbers have the letter 'e' in their name: onE, thrEE, fivE, sEvEn, ninE…
A Möbius strip has only one surface and one edge. If you cut it down the middle, it stays in one piece.
The most common letter in the English language is 'E'. The least common is 'Q'.
'Strengths' is the longest English word with only one vowel.
The word 'set' has the most definitions in the English language — over 430 different meanings.
There is no word in the English language that rhymes with 'orange', 'silver', 'month', or 'purple'.
The dot over the letters 'i' and 'j' is called a 'tittle'.
Your heart beats about 1,00,000 times per day and pumps about 7,570 litres of blood.
Humans share 60% of their DNA with bananas. We share 98.7% of our DNA with chimpanzees.
The human body replaces most of its cells every 7 to 10 years. Your bones fully renew themselves every decade.
Your fingerprints form in the womb and are unique to you — even identical twins have different fingerprints.
Goosebumps are a leftover reflex from our ancestors — it made their fur stand up to appear larger and warmer.
Cows have best friends and become stressed when separated from them. They also have regional accents.
The total weight of all the ants on Earth is estimated to roughly equal the total weight of all humans.
A snail can sleep for 3 years straight during periods of drought.
Elephants are the only animals that cannot jump. They are also the only non-human animals known to have death rituals.
The shortest war in history lasted 38 to 45 minutes — between Britain and Zanzibar on 27 August 1896.
A group of pugs is called a 'grumble'. A group of hippos is called a 'bloat'. A group of flamingos is a 'flamboyance'.
Hot water freezes faster than cold water under certain conditions. Scientists still debate exactly why.
The average cloud weighs about 500,000 kg — the weight of roughly 100 elephants.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping so they don't drift apart from each other. This is called 'rafting'.
The total length of blood vessels in a single human body, if laid end to end, would circle the Earth about 2.5 times.
Strawberries are not actually berries, but bananas are. Botanically, berries develop from a single flower with one ovary.
Scotland's national animal is the Unicorn — a mythical creature symbolizing purity, innocence, power, and independence.
The Eiffel Tower can grow by up to 15 cm during the summer. Heat causes the iron to expand, making the tower taller.
A 'jiffy' is an actual unit of time. It is the time it takes for light to travel one centimeter in a vacuum, about 33.36 picoseconds.
Honeybees can recognize human faces. They use the same part of their brain that humans use for face recognition.
There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way. Earth has about 3 trillion trees, while the Milky Way has 100-400 billion stars.
The heart of a blue whale is so big that a small child could swim through its arteries.
Sloths can hold their breath for up to 40 minutes underwater — longer than dolphins can hold theirs.
A group of porcupines is called a 'prickle'. A group of hedgehogs is called an 'array'.
Cows produce more milk when listening to relaxing music. Studies show they prefer slow, soothing tunes over fast ones.
The national flag of Nepal is the only flag in the world that is not a rectangle or a square. It is shaped like two stacked triangles.
An ostrich's eye is bigger than its brain, making it the largest eye of any land animal.
The shortest commercial flight in the world lasts just 57 seconds. It connects two islands in Scotland, flying 2.7 km.
Pineapples take about two years to grow. The fruit is actually a cluster of hundreds of tiny berries fused together.
A group of giraffes is called a 'tower' — a fitting name for the tallest animals on Earth.
Crocodiles cannot stick their tongues out. Their tongues are attached to the roof of their mouth by a membrane.
An apple floats in water because it is 25% air, making it less dense than water.
The human eye can see a candle flame from up to 2.4 kilometers away in complete darkness.
The opposite sides of a standard dice always add up to seven.
Earth's rotation is gradually slowing down. 4.5 billion years ago, a day on Earth was only about 6 hours long.
Mount Everest is growing by about 4 millimeters every year due to the tectonic plates pushing against each other.
Dolphins sleep with one eye open. Only half of their brain sleeps at a time so they can watch for predators and breathe.
The longest English word without a standard vowel is 'rhythms'.
The Amazon River discharges more water into the ocean than the next seven largest independent rivers combined.
Penguins propose to their mates by giving them a pebble. If the female accepts, they become partners for life.
The Olympic gold medal is actually made mostly of silver. It must contain at least 6 grams of gold, but the rest is solid silver.
Pluto's entire surface area is smaller than the total land area of Russia.
A flea can jump up to 200 times its own body length. If a human could do that, they could jump over the Eiffel Tower.
The Great Barrier Reef is the largest living structure on Earth, so massive it can be seen from space.
Neptune has the strongest winds in the solar system, reaching speeds of over 2,000 km/h.
A group of rhinos is called a 'crash'. A group of zebras is called a 'dazzle'.
Sloths take two weeks to digest their food. Their extremely slow metabolism helps them survive on very few calories.
The shortest full sentence in the English language is 'Go!'
Pineapples contain an enzyme called bromelain that digests protein. When you eat pineapple, it is literally eating you back.
Bats are the only mammals capable of true and sustained flight. 'Flying' squirrels only glide.
A day on Mars is about 24 hours and 37 minutes, which is remarkably close to a day on Earth.
A group of lizards is called a 'lounge'. A group of ferrets is called a 'business'.
The word 'robot' comes from the Czech word 'robota', which means forced labor or hard work.
The Milky Way and Andromeda galaxies are expected to collide in about 4.5 billion years, eventually merging into one galaxy.
Peanuts are not nuts; they are legumes, making them more closely related to beans and peas than to walnuts or almonds.
Hippopotamus milk is bright pink. It contains two pigments that give it a distinct strawberry-milk color.
Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a giant storm that has been raging for at least 350 years and is larger than Earth.
Seahorses are the only animal species in which the male gives birth to the offspring.
The world's smallest mammal is the bumblebee bat, which weighs less than a penny.
The human body has enough DNA to stretch from the Sun to Pluto and back — 17 times over.
Venus is the only planet in our solar system that spins backwards compared to most other planets.
Rubber bands last longer when refrigerated. The cold temperature prevents the rubber from degrading quickly.
A hummingbird weighs less than a penny, yet its heart beats over 1,200 times a minute.
'Queue' is the only English word that is pronounced the same way even if the last four letters are removed.
The Himalayas are still rising by about 1 centimeter per year because the Indian tectonic plate is pushing into Asia.
Croissants were actually invented in Austria, not France. They were originally called 'kipferl'.
There are more than 1,500 active volcanoes on Earth, and about 50 to 70 of them erupt every year.
A group of bears is called a 'sleuth'. A group of monkeys is called a 'barrel'.
The word 'nerd' was first coined by Dr. Seuss in his 1950 book 'If I Ran the Zoo'.
Koala fingerprints are so similar to human fingerprints that they have confused crime scene investigators.
There are more bacteria in your mouth than there are people on Earth.
An inch of rainwater is equal to about 10 inches of snow, depending on the snow's density.
A group of frogs is called an 'army'. A group of toads is called a 'knot'.
A single strand of spaghetti is called a 'spaghetto'.
A group of kangaroos is called a 'mob'. A group of whales is called a 'pod'.
The star Vega will become the new North Star in about 12,000 years due to Earth's wobbling axis.
The world's oldest known living tree is over 5,000 years old. It is a bristlecone pine located in California.
The 'smell of rain' has a name: petrichor. It comes from oils released by plants and the smell of wet soil.
The driest place on Earth is the Atacama Desert in Chile. Some parts have had no recorded rainfall in hundreds of years.
The first chocolate bar was made in 1847 by Joseph Fry in England. Before that, chocolate was only consumed as a drink.
A compass points north because Earth's core is made mostly of iron and nickel, creating a giant magnet.
The largest desert on Earth is Antarctica. A desert is defined by its low precipitation, not its temperature.
A group of apes is called a 'shrewdness'. A group of ravens is called an 'unkindness'.
The name 'Wendy' was created by J.M. Barrie for the play Peter Pan. Before that, it was rarely used as a name.
The first product to have a barcode scanned was a pack of Wrigley's Juicy Fruit gum in 1974.
The average lead pencil can draw a line 35 miles long or write roughly 45,000 words.
The word 'typewriter' can be typed using only the top row of a QWERTY keyboard.
The Earth's core is as hot as the surface of the Sun, reaching temperatures of about 5,500°C.
A group of finches is called a 'charm'. A group of hummingbirds is also called a 'charm'.
Cats sleep for about 70% of their lives. When they are awake, they spend a third of that time grooming themselves.
The average person's left hand does 56% of the typing when using a keyboard.
There are 293 different ways to make change for a US dollar using coins.
A group of starlings is called a 'murmuration'. They fly in massive, shifting shapes to confuse predators.
The longest place name on Earth is in New Zealand: Taumatawhakatangihangakoauauotamateaturipukakapikimaungahoronukupokaiwhenuakitanatahu.
A group of rattlesnakes is called a 'rhumba'.
A group of geese on the ground is called a 'gaggle', but a group of geese in the air is called a 'skein'.
The 'ZIP' in ZIP code stands for Zone Improvement Plan.
A group of trout is called a 'hover'. A group of sharks is called a 'shiver'.
The average person falls asleep in 7 minutes.
The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was first coined by John McCarthy in 1956 at a conference at Dartmouth College.
The first person to reach the South Pole was Roald Amundsen in 1911. He beat Robert Falcon Scott by just a few weeks.
The first Olympic Games were held in 776 BC in ancient Greece. The only event was a footrace called the 'stadion'.
The word 'salary' comes from the Latin word for salt, because Roman soldiers were sometimes paid in salt, which was highly valuable.
The first webcam was used at Cambridge University to monitor a coffee pot so people wouldn't walk down the hall for empty coffee.
The longest word in the English language is 45 letters long: pneumonoultramicroscopicsilicovolcanoconiosis. It's a type of lung disease.
A group of unicorns is called a 'blessing'.
The 'D' in D-Day stands for 'Day'. It was a military term used to designate the day an operation was to begin.
A group of peacocks is called an 'ostentation'. A group of pugs is called a 'grumble'.
The 'c' in the famous equation E=mc² stands for 'celeritas', which is Latin for swiftness or speed.
The world's largest waterfall is actually underwater. The Denmark Strait cataract in the Atlantic Ocean drops 3,505 meters.
The first CD pressed in the United States was Bruce Springsteen's 'Born in the USA'.
The word 'muscle' comes from the Latin word 'musculus', which means 'little mouse' because Romans thought flexing muscles looked like mice moving under the skin.
A group of parrots is called a 'pandemonium'.
The first computer mouse was made of wood. It was invented by Doug Engelbart in 1964.
The human eye blinks an average of 4,200,000 times a year.
A group of rabbits is called a 'colony', while a group of hares is called a 'drove'.
The first alarm clock could only ring at 4 AM. It was invented by Levi Hutchins in 1787 because he wanted to wake up early.
The 'spot' on 7-Up's logo comes from its inventor, Charles Leiper Grigg, who had red eyes because he was an albino.
A blink of an eye lasts about 300 to 400 milliseconds. You blink about 15-20 times per minute.
The longest hiccuping spree lasted 68 years. Charles Osborne started hiccuping in 1922 and didn't stop until 1990.
The Infinity symbol was originally used in ancient India and Tibet as a representation of perfection and dualism.
The human body has enough carbon to make 9,000 pencils.
The electric chair was invented by a dentist, Alfred P. Southwick, who saw how quickly electricity killed animals.
A day on Jupiter is only about 10 hours long. Despite being the largest planet, it spins the fastest.
The average person walks the equivalent of 5 times around the Earth in their lifetime.
The inventor of the Pringles can, Fredric Baur, was so proud of his invention that his ashes were buried in one when he died.
Tardigrades, or water bears, can survive the vacuum of space, extreme radiation, and temperatures from -272°C to 150°C.
The speed of light is exactly 299,792,458 meters per second. Nothing in the universe travels faster.
Magnets have north and south poles. If you break a magnet in half, you get two new magnets, each with its own north and south pole.
Absolute zero (-273.15°C) is the lowest possible temperature where all atomic motion stops completely.
The Leaning Tower of Pisa leans because its foundation was built on soft ground that couldn't support its weight.
Helium is the only element that was discovered in space before it was found on Earth. It was found in the Sun's spectrum in 1868.
The chemical symbol for gold is 'Au', which comes from the Latin word 'aurum', meaning 'shining dawn'.
Your saliva helps break down starches into sugars before you even swallow. That's why bread tastes sweeter the longer you chew it.
A falling raindrop is shaped more like a hamburger bun than a teardrop due to air resistance.
Galileo's middle finger is preserved in a museum in Florence, Italy.
A yoctosecond is a trillionth of a trillionth of a second, used to measure the time it takes light to cross a proton.
Chalk is made from the remains of tiny, ancient sea creatures called foraminifera.
Quicksand is just ordinary sand with too much water in it. It cannot suck you under completely; you will float.
You can't taste food without saliva. Food chemicals must dissolve in saliva to reach your taste receptors.
Cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis. The sound is just gas bubbles popping in the joint fluid.
If you spin a raw egg and stop it briefly, it will keep spinning. A boiled egg will stay stopped.
A tiger's stripes are as unique as human fingerprints; no two tigers have the exact same pattern.
Elephants are the only animals with four forward-facing knees.
A blue whale's tongue weighs as much as an entire elephant.
Frogs cannot swallow with their eyes open. They use their eyes to push food down their throats.
A shrimp's heart is located in its head.
Slugs have four noses — two for smelling and two for breathing.
Koalas sleep up to 22 hours a day to conserve energy because their eucalyptus diet is very low in nutrients.
A housefly hums in the key of F because its wings beat at a frequency that matches that musical note.
Camel milk doesn't curdle, which allows it to stay fresh much longer than cow milk in the desert heat.
Rats laugh when you tickle them, but their laugh is at a frequency humans cannot hear without special equipment.
A group of goldfish is called a 'troubling'.
A group of turkeys is called a 'rafter'.
Oysters can change their gender multiple times throughout their lives, switching back and forth based on temperature.
Armadillos always give birth to four identical babies, all of the same gender, that develop from a single egg.
Goats have rectangular pupils that give them a 340-degree field of vision, helping them watch for predators.
A group of vultures is called a 'committee' when resting, and a 'kettle' when flying.
A group of cockroaches is called an 'intrusion'.
A group of kittens is called a 'kindle'.
A group of mice is called a 'mischief'.
A group of penguins in the water is called a 'raft', but on land, they are called a 'waddle'.
A group of tigers is called a 'streak'.
A group of camels is called a 'caravan'.
A group of cheetahs is called a 'coalition'.
A group of foxes is called a 'skulk' or a 'leash'.
A group of gorillas is called a 'band' or a 'troop'.
A group of jaguars is called a 'shadow'.
A group of leopards is called a 'leap'.
A group of moles is called a 'labor'.
A group of otters is called a 'romp' on land and a 'raft' in the water.
A group of seals is called a 'harem' or a 'pod'.
A group of skunks is called a 'stench'.
A group of squirrels is called a 'scurry' or a 'dray'.
Mercury has no atmosphere, which means it has no wind or weather, just extreme heat and cold.
Mars has the tallest volcano in the solar system, Olympus Mons. It is nearly three times taller than Mount Everest.
Jupiter has 95 officially recognized moons, including Ganymede, which is larger than the planet Mercury.
Saturn's rings are mostly made of water ice and rock, ranging in size from tiny grains to houses.
Uranus rotates on its side, likely because a massive object knocked it over billions of years ago.
A year on Neptune is 165 Earth years long. It completed its first full orbit since being discovered in 2011.
Mars has sunsets that are blue. The fine dust in the Martian atmosphere causes blue light to scatter forward.
The largest known star, UY Scuti, is about 1,700 times the radius of the Sun.
If two pieces of the same type of metal touch in the vacuum of space, they will permanently bond together. This is called cold welding.
The Voyager 1 spacecraft, launched in 1977, is the farthest human-made object from Earth.
Astronauts grow up to 5 centimeters taller in space because the spine expands without gravity compressing it.
It rains diamonds on Jupiter and Saturn. Lightning turns methane gas into soot, which hardens into diamonds as it falls.
A full NASA spacesuit costs about 12 million dollars, with most of the cost going into the backpack and control module.
The first artificial satellite, Sputnik 1, was launched by the Soviet Union in 1957 and orbited Earth for 3 months.
Halley's Comet visits Earth every 76 years and will next be visible from Earth in the year 2061.
The asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter is mostly empty space, not a crowded field of rocks like in the movies.
The Moon has moonquakes caused by Earth's gravitational pull. They are weaker than earthquakes but can last longer.
A black hole is a place in space where gravity is so strong that nothing, not even light, can escape from it.
One million Earths could fit inside the Sun because it is so massive.
The International Space Station orbits Earth once every 90 minutes, meaning astronauts see about 16 sunrises a day.
Space is completely silent because there is no air for sound waves to travel through.
The Sun makes up 99.86% of the total mass of the entire solar system.
India has 22 officially recognized languages and over 19,500 dialects spoken across the country.
The Banyan tree is India's national tree. Some banyan trees can have canopies so large they look like a small forest.
The game of Snakes and Ladders originated in ancient India as a moral lesson about good deeds leading to heaven and bad deeds to rebirth.
In ancient India, rulers used diamonds to decorate their armor and weapons because diamonds were believed to offer protection.
India exports software to over 90 countries around the world, making it a global technology hub.
The world's first university was Takshashila, established in 700 BC in ancient India. Over 60 subjects were taught there.
Ayurveda, the earliest school of medicine known to humans, was founded in India over 2,500 years ago.
The art of navigation was born in the river Sindh over 6,000 years ago. The word 'navigation' comes from the Sanskrit word 'Navgatih'.
Algebra, Trigonometry, and Calculus originated in India. The concept of trigonometry was first introduced by the Indian mathematician Aryabhata.
The world's highest cricket ground is in Chail, Himachal Pradesh, built in 1893 at an altitude of 2,444 meters.
India has the world's largest sundial, located in Jaipur. It measures 27 meters tall and tells the time with an accuracy of 2 seconds.
Jaisalmer Fort in Rajasthan is one of the largest living forts in the world, with a quarter of the city's population living inside it.
Mawsynram in Meghalaya is the wettest place on Earth, receiving an average annual rainfall of over 11,800 mm.
India's first rocket was so small and light that it was transported to the launchpad on a bicycle in 1963.
Lonar Lake in Maharashtra is the only hyper-velocity impact crater in basaltic rock anywhere in the world, created by a meteor.
India is the only country in the world to have an entire ocean named after it — the Indian Ocean.
India is the world's largest producer of spices, growing over 70% of the world's spice varieties.
The Jaipur foot, a rubber-based prosthetic leg for below-knee amputees, was invented in India and has helped millions worldwide due to its low cost.
The Khardung La pass in Ladakh is one of the highest motorable roads in the world, standing at 5,359 meters.
The only prime number that is even is 2. All other even numbers can be divided by 2, so they aren't prime.
There are 360 degrees in a circle because ancient Babylonians used a base-60 number system for astronomy.
The number 4 is the only number in English that is spelled with the same number of letters as its value.
'Forty' is the only number spelled in alphabetical order. 'One' is the only one spelled in descending alphabetical order.
A soccer ball is made up of 20 hexagons and 12 pentagons to create a perfect sphere.
The symbol for division (÷) is called an obelus.
The symbol for percentage (%) comes from the Italian phrase 'per cento', which gradually got shortened into the modern symbol.
The word 'mathematics' comes from the Greek word 'mathema', which means learning, study, or science.
The number 9 is magical: multiply any number by 9, and the sum of the digits of the new number will always equal 9 (e.g., 9x5=45, 4+5=9).
The name of the equals sign (=) was invented by Robert Recorde in 1557 because nothing is more equal than two parallel lines.
A quintillion has 18 zeros. A decillion has 33 zeros.
The number 7 is considered the most popular lucky number globally, possibly because there are 7 days of the week and 7 visible planets in antiquity.
Your nose and ears never stop growing throughout your entire life due to gravity and cartilage changes.
You produce about 25,000 quarts of saliva in your lifetime, enough to fill two swimming pools.
The small intestine is about 22 feet long, while the large intestine is only about 5 feet long.
A sneeze travels out of your mouth at over 160 km/h, which is why covering your mouth is so important.
Your fingernails grow four times faster than your toenails because your hands have better blood circulation.
You can't tickle yourself because your brain predicts the sensation and dampens the response.
Every day, your heart pumps enough blood to fill a standard swimming pool over the course of a year.
The liver can regenerate itself from just 25% of its tissue, making it the only internal organ that can completely regrow.
You are taller in the morning than in the evening because the cartilage in your spine compresses throughout the day due to gravity.
Human teeth are as hard as shark teeth, though ours are designed for chewing while theirs are for tearing.
The average person sheds about 40 pounds of skin in their lifetime, accounting for a lot of household dust.
The brain itself cannot feel pain, even though it processes pain signals from the rest of the body.
